title: 10 best herbal tinctures for headaches
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for headaches are concentrated liquid extracts of medicinal herbs, which are used to alleviate headaches through their natural healing properties. These tinctures are often made from herbs such as feverfew, ginger, and willow bark, which have anti-inflammatory, analgesic, and muscle-relaxant effects that can help reduce headache pain and inflammation. By using herbal tinctures, individuals can benefit from a more natural and holistic approach to managing headaches, reducing the need for over-the-counter or prescription medications that may have unwanted side effects. For example, a person who suffers from chronic tension headaches may find relief by taking a feverfew tincture daily, improving their quality of life by reducing pain and increasing their ability to focus on daily activities.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Black cohosh tincture helps with headaches because it contains phytoestrogens, which are plant-based compounds that can mimic the effects of estrogen in the body. Fluctuations in estrogen levels are known to trigger headaches and migraines in some individuals, particularly women. By regulating estrogen levels, black cohosh tincture can help alleviate headache symptoms. Additionally, black cohosh has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can further help reduce headache pain and inflammation. Its sedative effects may also promote relaxation and reduce tension, which can contribute to headache relief.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Black cohosh tincture can reduce inflammation, which is important for headaches as inflammation can irritate nerves and cause pain. The active constituents responsible for this property include triterpene glycosides and aromatic acids.
Analgesic: Black cohosh tincture has pain-relieving properties, which can help alleviate headache pain. The active constituents responsible for this property include salicylic acid and tannins.

Antispasmodic: Black cohosh tincture can relax muscles and reduce muscle spasms, which can help relieve tension headaches. The active constituents responsible for this property include isoferulic acid and cimicifugin.

Nervine: Black cohosh tincture has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help reduce the frequency and intensity of headaches. The active constituents responsible for this property include triterpene glycosides and flavonoids.

Hormonal balance: Black cohosh tincture can help regulate hormonal imbalances, which can contribute to headaches. The active constituents responsible for this property include formononetin and dalbergioid.
remedy_parts: Roots: Black cohosh roots are commonly used to make medicinal tinctures for headaches due to their rich content of triterpene glycosides and phenolic acids, which have been suggested to have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes of black cohosh contain similar active compounds as the roots, making them a valuable part of the plant for creating tinctures that may help alleviate headache symptoms.

Flowers: While less commonly used, black cohosh flowers can be included in tinctures, as they may contain beneficial compounds that can contribute to the plant's overall therapeutic effects on headaches.
Leaves: Black cohosh leaves are occasionally used in tinctures, as they may possess active constituents that can help reduce headache-related inflammation and provide pain relief.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried black cohosh roots and rhizomes in the fall season when mature.
Chop the harvested material into small pieces and dry them thoroughly in a warm oven or dehydrator for 2-3 days.
Combine one part dried black cohosh with one part high-proof vodka or grain alcohol in a clean glass jar and let it macerate for 6-8 weeks.
Shake the jar daily to ensure the liquid extracts the active compounds from the plant material and then strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container.
Store the tincture in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids in a cool dry place for up to 2 years while monitoring its potency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sweet flag tincture helps with headaches because it contains properties that can help reduce inflammation and relax muscles, which can be beneficial for tension headaches. Its active compounds, such as beta-asarone, have been shown to have analgesic and sedative effects, making it a popular natural remedy for headache relief. Additionally, sweet flag tincture may help improve circulation and reduce stress, both of which can contribute to headaches. Its soothing and calming properties can also help alleviate the symptoms of headaches and promote overall relaxation.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Sweet flag tincture's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and irritation in blood vessels, which can cause headaches. This is due to the presence of compounds like asarone and beta-asarone.

Analgesic: The tincture can help alleviate pain, making it effective in managing headaches. This is attributed to the presence of essential oils and other active constituents such as methyl eugenol and camphor.

Sedative: Sweet flag tincture can help relax the nervous system, reducing tension and providing relief from headaches. This is due to the presence of compounds like asarone, which has sedative effects.

Antispasmodic: The tincture can help relieve muscle spasms and tension, which can contribute to headaches. This is due to the presence of compounds like asarone and eugenol, which have antispasmodic properties.

Circulatory stimulant: Sweet flag tincture can improve blood flow, helping to alleviate headaches caused by poor circulation. This is due to the presence of essential oils and other active constituents, which can help dilate blood vessels and improve circulation.
remedy_parts: Rhizomes: The rhizomes of sweet flag plant contain various beneficial compounds, such as asarone, that can help to alleviate headaches by relaxing the blood vessels in the brain.
Leaves: The leaves of sweet flag plant contain essential oils and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties, making them useful for treating headaches.
Flowers: The flowers of sweet flag plant contain flavonoids and other compounds that can help to reduce inflammation and alleviate headache symptoms.
Seeds: The seeds of sweet flag plant contain essential oils and other compounds that can help to relax the muscles and alleviate tension headaches.
Buds: The buds of sweet flag plant contain high concentrations of beneficial compounds, including asarone and other volatile oils, that can help to relieve headache pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest acorus calamus roots in the fall or early spring when they are at their highest concentration of active compounds.
Dry the harvested roots for at least two weeks using air circulation and low humidity to prevent mold growth.
Chop the dried roots into small pieces and combine with 50% vodka as a solvent to make the tincture.
Steep the root mixture in the vodka for four to six weeks, shaking the container daily to facilitate extraction of active compounds.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove any sediment or impurities and transfer it to dark glass bottles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Indian hemp tincture helps with headaches because it contains natural compounds that have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. These compounds, such as cannabidiol (CBD) and tetrahydrocannabinol (THC), interact with the body's endocannabinoid system to regulate pain and inflammation. When applied topically or taken orally, the tincture can help reduce the severity and frequency of headaches by alleviating muscle tension, improving blood flow, and reducing inflammation in the affected area. Additionally, the herbal formula of Indian hemp tincture may provide a more holistic and natural approach to headache relief compared to conventional medications.
remedy_properties: Analgesic: Indian hemp tincture contains cannabinoids, such as cannabidiol (CBD) and tetrahydrocannabinol (THC), which interact with the body's endocannabinoid system to alleviate pain, reducing headache intensity.

Anti-inflammatory: The presence of cannabinoids and terpenes in Indian hemp tincture, like beta-caryophyllene, helps reduce inflammation in the central nervous system, which is often a contributing factor in headaches.
Anxiolytic: Indian hemp tincture can help alleviate anxiety and stress, which are common headache triggers; CBD and other cannabinoids found in the tincture contribute to its calming effects.
Vasodilatory: Certain cannabinoids in Indian hemp tincture, such as THC, can help dilate blood vessels, improving blood flow and reducing the risk of tension headaches and migraines.

Neuroprotective: Indian hemp tincture's antioxidant properties, provided by cannabinoids and terpenes, help protect nerve cells from damage and degeneration, which can contribute to the prevention and management of headaches.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in CBD, known for its anti-inflammatory properties, helping to alleviate headache symptoms.
Leaves: Contain medicinal compounds, used to make soothing teas and tinctures, providing relief for tension headaches.
Seeds: High in essential fatty acids, aid in reducing inflammation and provide necessary nutrients for overall brain health.
Roots: Historically used for headache relief, containing active compounds that help alleviate pain and reduce inflammation.
Stems: Packed with medicinal properties, used in tinctures to support vascular health and help reduce headache frequency.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality apocynum cannabinum leaves and stems at dawn or early morning for maximum potency.
Cleanse the plant material by rinsing it with filtered water and gently scrubbing off any dirt or debris.
Chop the plant material into small pieces and combine 50 grams of it with 500 milliliters of high-proof vodka in a large glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 6 weeks, shaking the jar every other day to ensure maximum extraction of beneficial compounds.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container and store it in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yanhusuo tincture helps with headaches because it contains alkaloids that can help relax and dilate blood vessels in the brain, improving blood flow and reducing the pressure that can cause headaches. It has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for centuries to treat various types of headaches, including tension headaches and migraines. Yanhusuo tincture may also help reduce inflammation and relieve pain by inhibiting the release of certain chemicals in the body. Its active compounds, such as tetrahydropalmatine, have been shown to have analgesic and sedative effects, making it a natural and effective remedy for headache relief.
remedy_properties: Analgesic: Yanhusuo tincture has pain-relieving properties that help alleviate headaches by reducing the sensitivity to pain, due to the presence of alkaloids such as tetrahydropalmatine and corydaline.
Anti-inflammatory: It helps reduce inflammation in the blood vessels surrounding the brain, which can cause headaches, due to the presence of anti-inflammatory compounds such as salicylic acid.
Vasodilatory: Yanhusuo tincture can widen blood vessels, improving blood flow and reducing the pressure that can cause headaches, due to the presence of alkaloids such as tetrahydropalmatine.
Sedative: It can help alleviate tension headaches by reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation, due to the presence of alkaloids such as tetrahydropalmatine and corydaline.
Antispasmodic: Yanhusuo tincture can help relieve headaches caused by muscle tension by reducing muscle spasms, due to the presence of alkaloids such as tetrahydropalmatine.
remedy_parts: Roots: Yanhusuo roots contain various alkaloids that have been shown to help reduce the frequency and severity of headaches.
Rhizomes: Yanhusuo rhizomes are rich in active compounds that can help alleviate tension and migraine headaches.
Flowers: Yanhusuo flowers contain anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe headaches caused by inflammation.
Seeds: Yanhusuo seeds have been used in traditional medicine to treat headaches, as they contain compounds that can help relax blood vessels and improve circulation.
remedy_recipe: Measure 50g of dried corydalis yanhusuo roots and grind them into a fine powder.
Combine the powdered root with 500ml of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for at least 14 days to allow extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass container.
Transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles and store them in the refrigerator.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tian ma tincture helps with headaches because it contains a powerful compound called gastrodin, which has been shown to effectively alleviate various types of headaches, including tension headaches and migraines. Gastrodin works by reducing inflammation and improving blood flow in the brain, thereby reducing the pressure and pain associated with headaches. Additionally, tian ma tincture has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for centuries to treat headaches and other neurological disorders, making it a safe and natural remedy for those seeking relief from headache symptoms.
remedy_properties: Analgesic: Tian ma tincture contains gallic acid and related compounds that have pain-relieving properties, which can help alleviate headaches.

Anti-inflammatory: The tincture has anti-inflammatory effects due to the presence of compounds like gastrodin, which can help reduce swelling and inflammation in the blood vessels, thereby easing headaches.
Vasodilatory: Tian ma tincture contains compounds like gastrodin and batatasin III, which can dilate blood vessels and improve blood flow, helping to relieve tension headaches and migraines.
Sedative: The tincture contains compounds like gastrodin and batatasin III that have sedative effects, which can help reduce anxiety and stress, thereby alleviating tension headaches.

Antioxidant: Tian ma tincture contains antioxidants like gallic acid and catechins, which can help protect the brain and nervous system from oxidative stress and inflammation, reducing the likelihood of headaches.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Tian Ma plant are commonly used in medicinal tinctures for headaches due to their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes of the Tian Ma plant are used in tinctures for headaches as they help to alleviate pain and reduce inflammation.
Leaves: The leaves of the Tian Ma plant are used in medicinal tinctures for headaches as they contain compounds that help to relax muscles and relieve tension.
Flowers: The flowers of the Tian Ma plant are used in tinctures for headaches as they have a calming effect and can help to reduce anxiety and stress.
Seeds: The seeds of the Tian Ma plant are used in medicinal tinctures for headaches as they contain compounds that help to improve blood flow and reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality tian ma roots weighing at least 20% of the total formula weight.
Clean and slice the harvested roots into small pieces to ensure efficient extraction.
Combine one part tian ma root with three parts 95% grain alcohol in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to macerate for four weeks, shaking the jar every day or two during this time.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another container discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree tincture helps with headaches because it contains active compounds that have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. These compounds work to reduce inflammation in the blood vessels surrounding the brain, which can help alleviate the pain and pressure associated with headaches. Additionally, the tincture can help to relax tense muscles in the neck and head, further reducing the frequency and intensity of headaches. The herbal extract may also help to improve circulation, which can promote healing and reduce the symptoms of headaches. Overall, maidenhair tree tincture offers a natural and effective way to manage headaches and promote overall health and well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Maidenhair tree tincture helps reduce inflammation, which is important for headaches as inflammation can cause or worsen headache pain. The active constituents responsible for this property include ginkgolides and bilobalide.

Analgesic: Maidenhair tree tincture has pain-relieving properties, which can help alleviate headache pain. The active constituents responsible for this property include flavonoids and terpene lactones.

Vasodilatory: Maidenhair tree tincture helps widen blood vessels, improving blood flow and reducing the risk of tension headaches caused by constricted blood vessels. The active constituents responsible for this property include ginkgolides and flavonoids.

Antioxidant: Maidenhair tree tincture has antioxidant properties, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, both of which can contribute to headaches. The active constituents responsible for this property include flavonoids and terpene lactones.

Anxiolytic: Maidenhair tree tincture has calming and anxiety-reducing properties, which can help alleviate tension headaches caused by stress and anxiety. The active constituents responsible for this property include ginkgolides and bilobalide.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain high levels of rosmarinic acid, which has been shown to help reduce inflammation and ease headache pain.
Stems: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, stems can help improve blood circulation and reduce tension, alleviating headaches.
Roots: Traditionally used in Chinese medicine, roots contain ginkgolides and bilobalide, which have been shown to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Seeds: High in essential fatty acids and antioxidants, seeds can help reduce inflammation and provide relief from headaches.
Bark: Contains tannins and other compounds that can help reduce inflammation and provide pain relief for headaches.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of fresh ginkgo biloba leaves or 50 grams of dried leaves.
Chop the ginkgo biloba into small pieces and combine with 500 milliliters of vodka at 40% abv.
Steep the mixture for 2 weeks in a dark glass container, shaking daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another container, discarding solids.
Store the tincture in a dark glass bottle with tight-fitting lid at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender tincture helps with headaches because it contains active compounds, such as linalyl acetate and linalool, which have been shown to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. When taken internally, these compounds can help to reduce inflammation and relax muscles, including those in the head and neck, which can help to alleviate tension headaches. Additionally, lavender has been shown to have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help to reduce stress and anxiety, two common triggers of headaches. By addressing both the physical and emotional causes of headaches, English lavender tincture can provide effective and natural relief.
remedy_properties: Analgesic: English lavender tincture has pain-relieving properties that can help alleviate headaches by reducing the sensation of pain, due to its active constituents like linalyl acetate and linalool.
Anti-inflammatory: It helps decrease inflammation in blood vessels, which can cause headaches, thanks to the presence of compounds like caryophyllene and apigenin.
Anxiolytic: Lavender tincture has calming effects that can help reduce stress and anxiety, common headache triggers, by interacting with GABA receptors in the brain.
Sedative: Its sedative properties can promote better sleep and relaxation, helping to prevent tension headaches and migraines, due to the active constituent, linalool.
Spasmolytic: English lavender tincture can help relax muscles and alleviate muscle tension, a common cause of headaches, by containing compounds like linalool and cineole.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in calming compounds, the flowers of English lavender are commonly used to make medicinal tinctures for headaches due to their soothing properties and ability to alleviate tension.

Leaves: Containing high levels of therapeutic oils, the leaves of English lavender are used in medicinal tinctures for headaches as they help reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, thus easing discomfort.

Buds: Packed with pain-relieving and anti-inflammatory agents, the buds of English lavender are ideal for making medicinal tinctures for headaches, as they effectively target the source of the pain and reduce swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried lavandula angustifolia flowers in abundance for the desired quantity of tincture.
Chop the harvested flowers into small pieces and mix with vodka or other high-proof spirit in a 1:4 ratio by weight.
Transfer the mixture to a clean glass jar and seal it tightly, allowing it to macerate for at least four weeks shaking daily.
After the maceration period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another glass container discarding the solids.
Store the final tincture in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids and label them clearly indicating the date of preparation and dosage instructions.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm tincture helps with headaches because it contains rosmarinic acid, which has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. These properties can help reduce the swelling of blood vessels surrounding the brain, one of the primary causes of headaches. Additionally, lemon balm has been shown to have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help alleviate tension-related headaches. Its soothing properties can also help reduce anxiety and promote better sleep, both of which can contribute to the onset of headaches. By taking lemon balm tincture, individuals may find relief from headaches and experience an overall improvement in their mood and well-being.
remedy_properties: Nervine relaxant: Lemon balm tincture helps alleviate headaches by promoting relaxation and reducing tension in the nervous system, due to its active constituents such as rosmarinic acid and eugenol.
Anxiolytic: It helps manage headaches by reducing anxiety and promoting calmness, thanks to its rosmarinic acid and citral content.
Anti-inflammatory: Lemon balm tincture can help decrease headache intensity by reducing inflammation, due to the presence of eugenol, rosmarinic acid, and citral.
Antispasmodic: It can help relieve headaches caused by muscle tension or spasms, as it contains active compounds that help relax muscles, such as rosmarinic acid and eugenol.
Antiviral: Lemon balm tincture can help manage headaches caused by viral infections, such as the common cold or flu, due to its active constituents like citral and rosmarinic acid.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of lemon balm are commonly used in medicinal tinctures for headaches due to their high concentration of rosmarinic acid, which has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
Flowers: The flowers of lemon balm contain flavonoids and essential oils that have been shown to have relaxant effects, making them useful in tinctures for tension headaches.
Stems: While not as potent as the leaves or flowers, the stems of lemon balm still contain beneficial compounds and can be included in tinctures to make use of the entire aerial part of the plant.
Seeds: The seeds of lemon balm contain essential oils and acids that can be used in tinctures to help alleviate headaches caused by stress and anxiety.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried melissa officinalis leaves and flowers at peak potency for optimal extraction.
Combine one part dried herb with four parts high-proof grain alcohol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 6-8 weeks to allow for thorough extraction of active compounds.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle and discard the solids.
Store the finished tincture in a cool dark place for up to two years, shaking well before use as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Catnip tincture helps with headaches because it contains nepetalactone, a compound that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and mild sedative properties. These properties can help to reduce muscle tension and soothe nerves, which can often be the cause of headaches. Additionally, catnip is known to have a calming effect on the body, helping to reduce stress and anxiety that can also contribute to headaches. By taking catnip tincture, the active compounds are quickly absorbed into the bloodstream, allowing for fast-acting relief from headache symptoms. It's important to note that while catnip tincture can be an effective natural remedy for headaches, it should not be used as a substitute for medical treatment, especially in cases of severe or chronic headaches.
remedy_properties: Nervine: Catnip tincture acts as a nervine, helping to soothe and calm the nervous system, which can be beneficial for headaches caused by stress or tension. The active constituents responsible for this property include nepetalactone and essential oils.

Anti-inflammatory: Catnip tincture has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in blood vessels, a common cause of headaches. The active constituents responsible for this property include iridoids, such as aucubin and catalpol.

Analgesic: Catnip tincture has analgesic properties, helping to alleviate pain associated with headaches. The active constituents responsible for this property include nepetalactone and essential oils.

Antispasmodic: Catnip tincture has antispasmodic properties, which can help relax muscles and alleviate tension headaches. The active constituents responsible for this property include nepetalactone and essential oils.

Anxiolytic: Catnip tincture has anxiolytic properties, helping to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, which can be beneficial for headaches caused by anxiety or stress. The active constituents responsible for this property include nepetalactone and essential oils.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the catnip plant contain nepetalactone, a compound that may help relieve headaches by promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation.

Flowers: Catnip flowers are rich in essential oils and antioxidants, making them a valuable addition to medicinal tinctures for headaches, as they may help reduce inflammation and provide pain relief.
Stems: The stems of the catnip plant contain medicinal compounds, including iridoid glycosides and essential oils, which may help alleviate headaches by reducing muscle tension and inflammation.
Roots: Catnip roots are a source of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which may help soothe headaches by reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh catnip leaves at peak potency in the morning after dew has evaporated ensuring optimal essential oil content.
Rinse freshly harvested catnip leaves with filtered water to remove dirt and debris then pat dry gently.
Combine 1 part dried catnip herb with 4 parts vodka or high-proof grain alcohol in a clean glass jar tightly sealed for 2 weeks.
Shake the jar daily for 14 days allowing the herbal mixture to macerate and extract essential oils from the plant material.
Strain the liquid tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another glass container discarding solids and storing the finished product in a dark glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop tincture helps with headaches because it contains passive phytochemicals, particularly secobarbituric acids, that have been shown to possess analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds work by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins, chemicals that cause inflammation and sensitivity to pain. Additionally, maypop tincture may help relax constricted blood vessels in the head, thereby alleviating the pressure that can lead to headaches. Its sedative effects can also help reduce tension and anxiety, which are common triggers of headaches. However, it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before taking any herbal remedies, especially if you are taking other medications or have a medical condition.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic: Maypop tincture has a calming effect on the nervous system, reducing tension and anxiety that can contribute to headaches. This property is due to the presence of active constituents such as passionflower alkaloids and flavonoids.

Analgesic: Maypop tincture can help alleviate pain associated with headaches by inhibiting the inflammation and transmission of pain signals in the body. Key active constituents responsible for this property include various alkaloids and flavonoids.

Anti-inflammatory: Maypop tincture reduces inflammation in the body, which can help alleviate headaches caused by inflammation or swelling in the head or neck. The anti-inflammatory effects are attributed to the presence of various flavonoids, such as vitexin and isovitexin.

Antispasmodic: Maypop tincture can help relax muscles and alleviate tension headaches by reducing muscle spasms and cramping. This property is due to the presence of passionflower alkaloids, such as harman and harmaline.

Sedative: Maypop tincture promotes relaxation and sleep, which can help reduce the frequency and severity of headaches. This property is due to the presence of various alkaloids and flavonoids, which have a sedative effect on the central nervous system.
remedy_parts: Roots: The maypop roots contain sedative and analgesic properties that can help alleviate headaches.
Leaves: Maypop leaves have anti-inflammatory and muscle-relaxing properties, making them useful for treating headaches.
Flowers: The flowers of the maypop plant contain flavonoids and other compounds that can help reduce inflammation and ease headache pain.
Seeds: Maypop seeds are rich in antioxidants and have been shown to have a calming effect, which can help reduce the severity of headaches.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh maypop flowers passiflora incarnata at dawn when dew is still present and flowers are fully open.
Clean and dry the harvested flowers immediately by gently blowing away any dirt or debris.
Create a menstruum of 70% vodka and 30% distilled water in a glass jar and add one part dried maypop flowers to two parts menstruum.
Steep the mixture for 4-6 weeks shaking daily allowing the active compounds to infuse into the menstruum.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter pressing out any remaining liquid and discarding solids.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat headaches with herbal tinctures is by using feverfew and ginkgo biloba. Feverfew is a potent anti-inflammatory that helps reduce muscle tension and restricts blood vessel dilation, both common headache triggers. Ginkgo biloba enhances vascular health, improving blood flow and alleviating headaches. A combination of these tinctures, taken three times a day, can provide effective relief for various headache types. Consult a healthcare professional for proper dosage and potential interactions with other medications.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to headaches that are treated with herbal tinctures include migraines, tension headaches, and cluster headaches. For migraines, feverfew and butterbur are commonly used herbs. Tension headaches can be alleviated with lavender, chamomile, and skullcap tinctures. Cluster headaches may respond to butterbur and feverfew, as well as melatonin and valerian root for their sedative properties. It is essential to consult with a healthcare provider before starting any new herbal treatment to ensure safety and efficacy.
